human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for air-transportation crew scheduling specificity, duty/rest and reserve coverage, dispatch and customs boundaries, movement and handoff evidence, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to crew schedule support, duty/rest legality posture, reserve and pairing options, route and aircraft dependencies, documentation readiness, and escalation packaging rather than generic dispatch or logistics scheduling.

Authority sources cover FAA/eCFR flightcrew duty and rest, Part 121 dispatch and flight release, load manifest and flight plan record disposition, flight attendant staffing and rest, FAA dispatcher-role guidance, and CBP APIS crew manifest documentation.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope recommendations, missing prerequisites, demand exceeding capacity, conflicting priorities, final-approval pressure, movement handoff documentation, route disruption with compliance impact, dispatch and safety requests, customs documentation gaps, terminology normalization, source-policy conflict, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/air-transportation-and-airports/crew-scheduling-support-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent dispatch, customs/compliance, maintenance, warehouse, customer operations, OCC, crew tracking, and labor relations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated crew scheduling support specialist for evidence-backed schedule, capacity, allocation, readiness, and escalation recommendations in air transportation and airports.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
